title: Loop A equity: single-name regime-gated audits starve at the WF event floor — fix baseline windowing + route audits to the pooled universe
source: Loop A ticks 1-2 (2026-07-03): both DA-approved proposals died pre-subprocess at status=event_floor
why: Two independent failures with one structure. Tick 1 (BEAR_QUIET, bollinger 15/2.0): 73 in-regime events vs floor 599. Tick 2 (BULL_QUIET, cusum default 2.0): 364 vs 599 — despite the dossier baseline citing 803. Root causes: (1) WINDOW MISMATCH — dossier baselines are measured over the ticker's FULL history (ABT: ~41y) while the audit config restricts to 2006-2026 (~20y), so the hypothesizer designs against event counts ~2x what the audit will see; the DA even enforced 'use the measured baseline' which is the wrong target. (2) STRUCTURAL — single-name x regime-gate (16-68% of bars) x sparse event primary on D1 arithmetically cannot clear wf_event_floor(3, 1500)=599 within a 20y window for most cells; the floor exists for good reason (WF geometry), so the fix is more events, not a lower bar. The M3 pooled cross-section is the natural resolution: 35 names x ~364 regime-gated sparse events ~= 12.7k pooled events, asynchronous across names (effective-N scales, unlike the falsified dense-primary pooling).
value_rationale: Blocks Loop A on equities entirely: every regime-conditional sparse-primary proposal will starve at audit regardless of quality. Unblocking it combines the two validated directions (sparse event primaries + pooled cross-section).
scope: Fix dossier baseline windowing: measure primary_baseline_summary n_events over the AUDIT config window (2006+), or report both full-history and audit-window counts; update hypothesizer payload guidance to design against the audit-window number

Design decision (spec-level): extend phase5.run_proposal to audit equity proposals against the POOLED M3 universe (regime gate per name, pooled WF via _run_one_pool) instead of single-name run_backtest — an equity-specific audit path
Interim cheap option: per-episode falsification criterion (already supported by the floor error message) for single-name audits
Re-run the T002D1 cusum/BULL_QUIET hypothesis under whichever path lands first — the hypothesis itself was DA-approved and remains untested (starved, not falsified)

history.reason: logged after ticks 1-2 both returned status=event_floor

Implementation plan written: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-03-pooled-audit-cross-section.md (6 tasks; pooled audit path Tasks 3-4, windowing fix Task 5, T002D1 re-audit Task 6; per-episode interim criterion deliberately superseded by the pooled path)

Task 4 landed: phase5/run_proposal.py gained build_transient_pooled_config / run_pooled_audit / grade_pooled_v1 + --pooled-universe CLI flag (commit 53bf528). Pooled event-floor check (sum of in-scope events vs wf_event_floor) gates the heavy subprocess; full run wires threshold_selection.method=fixed_ev + metrics.audit_effective_threshold so metrics_per_fold.json rows are directly comparable per (asset, fold, best_model) cell. Grading (pooled_v1) computes n_trades_total, nanmedian median_active_fold_sharpe over active (n_trades>=30) cells, and breadth (assets clearing 30 trades + positive aggregate sharpe); never auto-promotes -- status is event_floor or completed_pending_human_read only. Tests: tests/phase5/test_pooled_audit_path.py (7 cases). Full suite: 1327 tests, 0 failed, 19 skipped.
